title: pipeline.dlq
description: Published by the streaming-consumer when Cloud SQL is unhealthy (circuit breaker open) and a batch cannot commit. The batch is diverted here instead of being lost. A separate DLQ drainer (or recovery process) replays from this topic when Cloud SQL health restores. Distinct from the quarantine topic: this is infrastructure failure (recoverable), not data failure (tenant action needed).

dlq_id.description: Unique identifier for this DLQ entry. Used by the recovery process for idempotency.

reason.description: Why this batch was diverted to DLQ rather than committed to canonical.

reason_detail.description: Free-text additional context (probe error, last exception message). Optional.

tenant_id.description: Internal tenant UUID whose batch was diverted. All rows in the batch belong to one tenant (batched by tenant_id). Load-bearing identity.

tenant_display_code.description: Customer Master's authoritative external code for the tenant (tenants.display_code). Optional in the schema, but producers MUST populate it when publishing. Readability only, never a substitute for tenant_id.

batch.description: The batch that could not be committed.

trace_ids.description: Distinct trace_ids represented in this batch. Used by the recovery process to track which chunks are pending.

rows_ref.description: GCS URI of the serialized batch payload. The DLQ message does not carry rows inline (could be 500+ rows, 100s of KB each). The recovery process fetches from this URI when ready to retry. The tenant path segment is the internal tenant UUID.

mapping_version_ids.description: Distinct mapping_version_ids used to produce these rows. Replay must use the same versions, not current. Carries B1 pinning forward into recovery.

retry_attempts.description: How many times this batch has been retried via DLQ. Hard cap (default 5) enforced by the recovery process; exceeded entries surface as ops alerts.
